description:
Construct an Active Transportation Propensity Score (ATPS) index for San Francisco zones using UrbanSim data to identify optimal neighborhoods for micro-mobility investments. Using the starter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/active_transportation_index.ipynb and the HDF5 data store at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, calculate a composite spatial index by linking households and jobs to zones via buildings and parcels. 

For each zone, determine the parcel count, total households, total jobs, and zero-car households. Filter the dataset to exclude zones with fewer than 50 parcels or zero households. For the remaining zones, compute normalized components (0.0 - 1.0 range using Min-Max scaling) named hh_density_norm, job_density_norm, and zero_car_pct_norm based on household density, job density, and zero-car percentage. The final atp_score must be the equally weighted average of these three normalized components. 

Export the final dataframe, sorted descending by atp_score, to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/active_transit_scores.csv; the file must include the zone_id, raw components, normalized components, and the atp_score. Additionally, save a bar chart showing the three normalized components for the top 15 zones to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/top_atps_zones.png.
